title: 51% Attack
description: Also known as a majority attack. When one or a group of miners control greater than 50% of the network's mining hashrate or computational power.

title: Absolute Advantage
description: An economics concept in which one party has a direct advantage in efficiency in producing/providing a specific good or service over another party.

title: Active Management
description: An investing strategy employed by fund managers aiming to outperform an index or market in order to generate profits.

title: Actively Validated Services (AVS)
description: Systems that undergo continuous monitoring and validation to ensure they operate correctly.

title: Ad Hoc
description: A phrase of Latin origin that is used in modern English to mean 'for this purpose' or 'specifically for this.'

title: Address
description: String of text that designates the location of a particular wallet on the blockchain. Often a hashed version of a public key.

title: Aevo (AEVO)
description: A decentralized exchange focused on derivatives trading.

title: Airdrop
description: The distribution of digital assets to the public, either by virtue of holding a certain other token or simply by virtue of being an active wallet address on a particular blockchain.

title: Algorithm
description: A sequence of unambiguous instructions used for the purpose of solving a problem.

title: All or None Order (AON)
description: An order which once placed, must either be filled in its entirety or not at all. This prevents any partial filling of orders.

title: All-Time High (ATH)
description: The highest price that an asset has ever reached in its trading history.

title: Allocation
description: An allotment of tokens or equity, that may be earned, purchased, or set aside for a certain investor, team, group, organization, or other related entity.

title: Alpha
description: The first look at a product released by a team, consisting of the very first version of a primitive, basic software or product.

title: Altcoin
description: A cryptocurrency that is alternative to Bitcoin. Used to describe cryptocurrencies that are not Bitcoin.

title: Angel Investor
description: Wealthy investors that seek out opportunities to provide funding for entrepreneurs or start-up companies.

title: Anti Money Laundering (AML)
description: A framework consisting of legal and regulatory procedures to minimize and curb the flow of funds that are generated from illegal or dubious activities.

title: Application Programming Interface (API)
description: A collection of functions and procedures that allow users to interact/communicate with the data of an application or service, such as an exchange, to execute the features of the service programmatically.

title: Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC)
description: An Integrated Circuit customized for maximum performance in a particular use, rather than general-purpose use.

title: Arbitrage
description: Buying and selling of assets over different markets in order to take advantage of differing prices on the same asset.

title: ARC-20
description: A token standard for fungible tokens (colored coins) on the Bitcoin network.

title: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the intelligence displayed by machines or computer systems, simulating human-like cognitive processes.

title: ASIC-resistant
description: An ASIC-resistant cryptocurrency has its protocol and mining algorithm configured in such a way that using ASIC machines to mine the coin is either impossible or brings no significant benefit when compared to traditional GPU mining.

title: Ask Price
description: The lowest price a seller is willing to accept on their sell order when trading an asset on an exchange.

title: Asset Management
description: A system or method that helps individuals or companies manage assets. Either on behalf of their clients or their own.

title: Assets Under Management (AUM)
description: AUM (assets under management) is the market value of financial assets managed by an entity on behalf of clients, indicating influence and operational scale.

title: Asynchronous
description: Events in electronic systems that do not happen at the same time or speed, or happen independent of the main program flow.

title: Atomic Swap
description: Smart contract technology that enables the exchange of one cryptocurrency for another without using centralized intermediaries, such as exchanges.

title: Attack surface
description: Points in a software environment where an attacker can attempt to enter or extract data from the system.

title: Auction
description: A live event where assets or services are negotiated through a bidding process, which is often led by an auctioneer.

title: Automated Market Maker (AMM)
description: Automated Market Makers (AMMs) are a subset of decentralized exchange (DEX) protocols. They allow users to trade digital assets without the need for an intermediary.

title: B-Tokens
description: B-Tokens are the name for a range of fully-collateralized wrapped tokens minted by Binance.

title: Bags
description: The portfolio of coins and tokens one is holding. Often related to a poorly performing bag of assets that investors insist to hold.

title: Beacon Chain
description: The Beacon Chain is Ethereum’s proof-of-stake (PoS) layer where consensus is reached. It controls and coordinates the network of Ethereum stakers who help validate and secure the network in exchange for staking rewards.

title: Bear Flag Pattern
description: A pause in a strong downtrend that often leads to lower prices.

title: Bear Market
description: A negative trend in prices of a market.

title: Benchmark
description: A measurement standard which can be used to gauge the performance of a particular asset or investment portfolio.

title: BEP-1155
description: A token standard that optimizes the creation and management of digital assets on the BNB Chain.

title: BEP-20
description: A token standard on BNB Smart Chain (BSC).

title: BEP-721
description: A technical standard that defines a set of rules for the issuance of NFTs in the BNB Smart Chain ecosystem.

title: BEP-95
description: BEP-95 is a BNB Evolution Proposal that introduces a real-time burning mechanism to BNB Chain.

title: Beta (Coefficient)
description: A tool used to measure the volatility of an asset in comparison to the volatility of a specific portfolio or market index.

title: Beta (Release)
description: An early version of a program for users to test and for a team to get feedback. Beta stage comes after the Alpha stage.

title: Bid Price
description: In the context of financial markets, it is the value buyers offer for an asset, such as a commodity, security, or cryptocurrency.

title: Bid-Ask Spread
description: The difference in price between the lowest asking price and highest bid price on the order book for an asset.

title: Binance Ecosystem Fund (BEF)
description: An initiative by Binance to collaborate with partners who care about the blockchain/cryptocurrency ecosystem.

title: Binancian
description: A term referring to individuals who are actively involved with or contribute to the cryptocurrency exchange Binance.

title: Bitcoin
description: A cryptocurrency created by the pseudonymous developer(s) Satoshi Nakamoto. The first cryptocurrency, initially described as a 'Peer-to-Peer e-cash'.

title: Bitcoin Core
description: Leading implementation of the software that enables users to interact with the Bitcoin network. Initially released by Satoshi.

title: Bitcoin Dominance
description: The ratio of Bitcoin's market capitalization versus the sum of the market capitalizations of all cryptocurrencies.

title: Bitcoin Maximalists
description: Bitcoin Maximalists are individuals who believe that bitcoin is the only valuable cryptocurrency and the future of global finance.

title: Bitcoin Pizza
description: The first known transaction where Bitcoin was exchanged for a physical good. Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas.

title: Black Swan Event
description: An unexpected event that causes widespread ramifications.

title: Block
description: A computer file that stores transaction data. These can then be arranged in a linear sequence, which will form a blockchain.

title: Block Explorer
description: An online Blockchain webpage which allows users to browse information about blocks, transactions, balances, and transaction histories.

title: Block header
description: A section in a block containing metadata and a summary of the block's transactions. This is the information hashed when mining.

title: Block Height
description: The number of blocks in the chain between itself and the first block on that blockchain (genesis block or block 0).

title: Block Reward
description: The sum of coins awarded by the blockchain protocol to cryptocurrency miners for each successfully mined and validated block.

title: Blockchain
description: A decentralized, digitized ledger that records transaction information about a cryptocurrency in a chronological order.

title: Blockchain Charity Foundation
description: The world's first decentralized charity platform to advocate the concept of 'blockchain for social good'.

title: Bloom Filter
description: A data structure that can be used to inform the user whether a particular item is part of a set.

title: Blue-Chip Token
description: Blue-chip cryptos are coins that have withstood the test of time. These assets are established and reliably valuable.

title: BNB
description: BNB Chain's native token.

title: BNSOL
description: A liquid staking token that represents your staked SOL on Binance.

title: Bollinger Bands
description: A technical analysis indicator that measures market volatility. It's made of two sidelong bands and a simple moving average.

title: Bond
description: A type of fixed-income investment that represents a loan made to a borrower, typically a business or government.

title: Bounty
description: A reward posted by a group or individual to incentivize certain work, behavior (such as referrals), or development.

title: BRC-20 Tokens
description: BRC-20 is an experimental token standard that enables the minting and transferring of fungible tokens via the Ordinals protocol on the Bitcoin blockchain.

title: Break-Even Point (BEP)
description: The point where the total costs of an operation is equivalent to its current value or revenue.

title: Breakeven Multiple
description: The multiple of the current price by which an asset needs to appreciate in order to reach its previous all-time high.

title: Breakout
description: When the price of an asset moves outside of a defined range or pattern, typically by breaking out of a support or resistance area.

title: BscScan
description: A blockchain explorer designed for BNB Chain users.

title: BTC Wallet Address
description: A unique string of letters and numbers used to send and receive BTC transactions.

title: BUIDL
description: Originally derived from HODL, a term referring to keeping your heads down and focusing on building your product.

title: Bull Flag Pattern
description: A pause in a strong upward price move that often leads to higher prices.

title: Bull Market
description: A positive trend in prices of a market. It is widely used not only in the cryptocurrency space but also in the traditional markets.

title: Burner Wallet
description: A temporary crypto wallet for potentially risky interactions.

title: Buy Wall
description: A single huge buy order or the composition of multiple large buy orders at the same price in the order book of a particular market.

title: Candidate Block
description: A temporary block created by a mining node (miner) to add to the blockchain to receive the block rewards.

title: Candlestick
description: A graph representation of price action that displays the open, close, high, and low points within a certain period.

title: Capitulation
description: A period of strong selling activity, where investors give up their positions and sell their holdings as quickly as possible.

title: Censorship-resistance
description: The property of a cryptocurrency network that prevents any entity from altering transactions on it.

title: Central Bank
description: A financial institution that acts as a monetary authority and manages a states currency, interest rates, and money supply.

title: Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC)
description: A Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) is an officially sanctioned digital tender issued by a sovereign central bank. CBDCs can be either wholesale or retail.

title: Central Processing Unit (CPU)
description: The part of a computer responsible for interpreting instructions of computer programs and executing operations.

title: Centralized
description: When the planning and decision-making mechanisms are concentrated in a particular point within a system.

title: Centralized Exchange
description: Understand the world of centralized exchanges, traditional crypto marketplaces known for high liquidity, advanced trading tools, and risk considerations.

title: ChatGPT
description: ChatGPT is a language processing model developed by OpenAI. It represents a significant advancement in the field of natural language processing (NLP).

title: Cipher
description: A method for encrypting and decrypting messages. These can be divided into symmetric or asymmetric, according to their key model.

title: Circulating Supply
description: The best approximate number of cryptocurrency coins or tokens that are publicly available and circulating in the market.

title: Cloud
description: In computer science, a shared pool of resources, which are made available to multiple users through the Internet.

title: Coin
description: A cryptocurrency or digital cash that is independent of any other platform, which is used as an exchange of value.

title: Cold Storage
description: The practice of storing digital assets offline to mitigate risks.

title: Collateral
description: Pledging something of value against a loan to guarantee that the person taking the loan will complete repayment.

title: Colocation
description: A dedicated space in a data center belonging to stock exchanges which is shared with other entities such as high-frequency traders.

title: Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC)
description: US-based agency responsible for regulating the derivatives markets, which includes options, swaps, and futures contracts.

title: Compound Interest
description: Compound interest refers to the interest accumulated on the principal amount, in addition to the interest from previous periods; this allows you to maximize your earnings on the principal sum.

title: Confirmation Time
description: The interval needed for a blockchain transaction to be approved and added to the ledger.

title: Confluence
description: When multiple investment methods, technical indicators, or trading signals are combined to form a more reliable strategy.

title: Consensus Algorithm
description: Consensus algorithms are mechanisms that help different computers in a blockchain network agree on what's true, even if some of them aren't playing fair.

title: Consumer Price Index (CPI)
description: A Consumer Price Index (CPI) is a measure used to track the effects of inflation over a period of time.

title: Contango and Backwardation
description: Contango and backwardation are terms used to describe the relationship between the futures price of a commodity and its expected future spot price. They are useful metrics for trading, hedging and arbitrage.

title: Copy Trading
description: Copy trading is the process of emulating the successful trades of experienced investors.

title: Counterparty Risk
description: Counterparty risk refers to the risk that one party involved in a financial transaction may default or fail to fulfill its contractual obligations, causing financial losses to the other party.

title: Credentials
description: Personal information. Examples include username, password, email address, qualifications and many more.

title: Cross-Chain Bridges
description: Blockchain bridges connect different blockchain networks, enabling the movement of digital assets across networks and facilitating interoperability in the crypto space.

title: Crypto ETFs
description: Crypto ETFs allow investors to gain exposure to the price movements of cryptocurrencies without owning, or managing, them directly.

title: Crypto Fear and Greed Index
description: The Crypto Fear and Greed Index is a tool that uses several indicators to help measure the overall cryptocurrency market sentiment on a 0-100 score range.

title: Crypto Protocol
description: A crypto protocol is a set of rules and procedures that govern behavior in a decentralized network.

title: Crypto Winter
description: The term crypto winter refers to an extended period of declining or stagnant prices and negative sentiment in the cryptocurrency market.

title: Cryptocurrency
description: A digital currency that is secured by cryptography to work as a medium of exchange within a peer-to-peer (P2P) economic system.

title: Cryptography
description: The science of using mathematical theories and computation in order to encrypt and decrypt information.

title: Custody
description: Refers to the holding of assets on behalf of a client. Can also refer to the ownership of one's funds or assets.

title: Daemon
description: A process operating in the background waiting for a specific event or condition in order to be activated.

title: Danksharding
description: Danksharding brings a new approach to sharding and plays a crucial role in optimizing data management and transaction processing within the Ethereum network.

title: Dead Cat Bounce
description: A brief recovery in the price of a declining asset that is shortly followed by a continuation of the downtrend.

title: Death Cross
description: A pattern that indicates the potential for a market downturn.

title: Decentralized Application (DApp)
description: DApps are applications that run on a P2P network of computers rather than a central database. Users can freely connect to DApps using crypto wallets.

title: Decentralized Autonomous Cooperative (DAC)
description: An organization that is controlled by shareholders rather than a central authority.

title: Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO)
description: A system of hard-coded rules that define which actions a decentralized organization will take.

title: Decentralized Exchange (DEX)
description: An exchange which does not require users to deposit funds to start trading and does not hold the funds for the user. Instead, users trade directly from their own wallets.

title: Decentralized Finance (DeFi)
description: The ecosystem comprised of decentralized financial applications developed on top of blockchain networks.

title: Decentralized Indexes
description: A decentralized index is a type of investment vehicle that tracks the performance of multiple cryptocurrencies or digital assets traded on decentralized exchanges.

title: Decryption
description: The act of reverting an encryption process so that unreadable data (ciphertext) can be converted into readable data (plaintext).

title: Deep Web
description: The part of the web that is somehow hidden, i.e., not indexed by Google and other traditional web search engines.

title: Delisting
description: The removal of an asset from an exchange either as a request from the project team or as a decision made by the exchange.

title: Depeg
description: Learn about causes, effects, and preventive measures to avoid a depeg and ensure stablecoin reliability and trust in the cryptocurrency market.

title: Depression
description: An extended and severe downturn in economic activity.

title: Design Flaw Attack
description: An attack in which a malicious user purposely creates a smart-contract, decentralized market, or other software with knowledge of certain flaws in order to trick individuals interacting within the permissionless environment.

title: Diamond Hands
description: Diamond hands refers to holding a financial asset and not selling it, regardless of its volatility.

title: Difficulty
description: The effort required to mine a block.

title: Difficulty Bomb
description: The term 'difficulty bomb' denotes the increase in mining difficulty in Ethereum, as part of its migration to Proof of Stake.

title: Digital Asset
description: Something created, stored, and accessed in a digital form.

title: Digital Signature
description: A cryptographic tool to verify the authenticity and integrity of digital data, ensuring the security of data communication and cryptocurrency transactions.

title: Direct Market Access (DMA)
description: A technology that provides investors direct access to order books without the need for brokers.

title: Divergence
description: When the market price of an asset and a technical indicator (e.g. RSI, Volume, MACD) are heading in opposite directions.

title: Diversification
description: The allocation of funds across different types of assets and jurisdictions in order to reduce the overall risks.

title: Do Your Own Research (DYOR)
description: A valuable piece of advice. It's always wise to research a coin or token yourself instead of following what others say.

title: Dollar Cost Averaging (DCA)
description: Investing fixed dollar amounts over regular periods of time regardless of the price of the asset.

title: Dot Plot
description: A simple type of chart where each data point shows up as a dot.

title: Double Spending
description: When a given amount of coins are spent more than once. Usually as a result of a race attack or a 51% attack.

title: Eclipse Attack
description: When the majority of peers on the network are malicious and monopolize the network in order to prevent specific nodes from receiving information from honest nodes.

title: Efficient Market Hypothesis (EMH)
description: An economic theory stating financial markets reflect all available information on the price of assets at any time.

title: EigenLayer
description: A blockchain protocol that introduced the concept of "restaking collective" for Ethereum.

title: EIP
description: Ethereum Improvement Proposals that describe suggested upgrades and new features in detail.

title: EIP-1559
description: A 2021 upgrade that changed Ethereum’s fee system.

title: EIP-3074
description: An improvement proposal for Ethereum aimed at improving transaction authorization processes.

title: EIP-4844
description: EIP-4844, also known as proto-danksharding, is a proposed upgrade that focuses on reducing Ethereum’s fees and increasing transaction throughput.

title: EIP-7251
description: A proposal to modify Ethereum's staking by increasing the Maximum Effective Balance.

title: EIP-7702
description: A proposal designed to enhance Externally Owned Accounts (EOAs) on Ethereum.

title: Elasticity
description: Measures the responsiveness of one variable to changes in another variable.

title: Encryption
description: Conversion of information or data into a secure code in order to prevent unauthorised access to the information or data.

title: Endogenous Variable
description: A variable in an economic model that is directly related to other variables in the same model.

title: Enterprise Ethereum Alliance (EEA)
description: The industry’s first global standards organization to deliver an open, standards-based architecture and specification to accelerate the adoption of Enterprise Ethereum.

title: ERC-1155
description: Discover the versatility of ERC-1155 tokens. Ideal for gaming, digital collectibles and managing multiple token types efficiently.

title: ERC-20
description: A technical standard used to issue and implement tokens on the Ethereum blockchain proposed in November 2015 by Fabian Vogelsteller.

title: ERC-404
description: An experimental token standard blending fungibility and non-fungibility.

title: ERC-721
description: An Ethereum based non-fungible token. This means that each token is unique and as a result, not interchangeable.

title: ETF
description: ETFs are exchange-traded funds, in this article we explore their role in the cryptocurrency market: benefits, impacts, and challenges for investors and the financial ecosystem.

title: Ethereum Classic
description: Ethereum Classic (ETC) emerged from the Ethereum hard fork that happened as a result of the 2016 DAO hack. Unlike ETH, ETC still uses Proof of Work.

title: Ethereum Foundation
description: A non-profit organization dedicated to the development, improvement, and promotion of the Ethereum blockchain.

title: Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)
description: The Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM) is a Turing-complete programmable machine that executes smart contracts, which are typically coded in Solidity.

title: Exchange
description: A marketplace for cryptocurrencies where users can buy and sell coins.

title: Exogenous Variable
description: A variable in an economic model that impacts the model as an outside factor.

title: Exponential Moving Average (EMA)
description: A moving average used in trading that gives more weight to recent data points.

title: Externally Owned Account (EOA)
description: A type of account on the Ethereum blockchain controlled by a private key.

title: Fakeout
description: A situation where a trader enters a position betting on a price movement that quickly reverses or ultimately doesn’t happen.

title: Falling Knife
description: An asset whose price is dropping sharply and rapidly.

title: Falling Wedge Pattern
description: A chart pattern of converging trend lines that often lead to a bullish reversal.

title: Fan Tokens
description: Fan tokens are digital assets that are created by sports teams, clubs or brands to increase fan engagement and create new revenue streams.

title: Fear Of Missing Out (FOMO)
description: The feeling of fear and anxiety that you might be missing out on a potentially profitable opportunity.

title: Fear, Uncertainty and Doubt (FUD)
description: A strategy used to spread fear and insecurity among customers, traders, or investors.

title: Fiat
description: Money that a government has declared to be legal tender.

title: Fibonacci Retracement
description: A technical analysis tool used by traders to find support and resistance levels.

title: Fill Or Kill Order (FOK)
description: A buy or sell order which must be executed immediately in its entirety or else it will be cancelled.

title: Finality
description: The assurance or guarantee that completed (cryptocurrency) transactions cannot be altered, reversed or canceled.

title: First-Mover Advantage (FMA)
description: The competitive advantage of the first project to bring a service or product into a new and unexplored market or industry.

title: Fiscal Policy
description: Describes how authorities adjusts the tax rates of a country, influencing how public funds should be collected and used.

title: Flappening
description: The name to describe the moment when Litecoin (LTC) surpassed Bitcoin Cash (BCH) in terms of market capitalization.

title: Flashbots
description: Explore Flashbots, a research & development organization focused on refining MEV-related blockchain solutions on Ethereum.

title: Flippening
description: The name to describe the moment when/if Ethereum (ETH) surpasses Bitcoin (BTC) in terms of market capitalization.

title: Flow Variable
description: A measurement of something over a specific period of time.

title: Flux (FLUX)
description: A decentralized cloud infrastructure network for scalable Web3 applications.

title: Forced Liquidation
description: When a trader's leveraged position is forcibly closed as a result of it not fulfilling the necessary margin requirements.

title: Forex (FX)
description: Forex stands for Foreign Exchange Markets. It's a global market for the trading of fiat currencies.

title: Fork
description: A fork is a divergence in the blockchain network by creating a split in the blockchain's transaction history. There are two types of forks - soft and hard.

title: Formal Verification
description: Using mathematically rigorous proofs to ensure certain properties of cryptographic algorithms and blockchain mechanisms.

title: Fraud Proof
description: A fraud proof is a cryptographical evidence that a verifier submits to challenge a transaction's validity. They are widely used for blockchain scalability.

title: Fren
description: An online slang term used to address someone in a friendly way.

title: Full Node
description: A computer that fully implements the entirety of rules of an underlying blockchain network and completely validates transactions and blocks on a blockchain.

title: Fully Diluted Valuation (FDV)
description: Fully Diluted Valuation tells us what a project’s market value could be in the future.

title: Fundamental Analysis (FA)
description: Evaluating an asset based on its underlying characteristics and traits as an effort towards arriving at an intrinsic value of the asset.

title: Funding Fees
description: Periodic payments between long and short traders in perpetual futures markets.

title: Fungibility
description: The property of an asset whose individual units are indistinguishable from each other in terms of value and functionality.

title: Futures Contract
description: A standardized version of forward contracts that are used as a legal agreement to buy or sell an asset in the future at an agreed upon price and date.

title: GameFi
description: GameFi is a section of DeFi that merges blockchain technology and gaming, offering play-to-earn incentives through NFTs and cryptocurrencies.

title: Gas
description: A pricing mechanism that calculates the costs of smart contracts operations and transaction fees.

title: Gas Limit
description: The maximum price a cryptocurrency user is willing to pay as a fee when sending a transaction, or performing a smart contract function.

title: GDP Deflator
description: A measure that shows how much the prices in a country have increased over time.

title: General Public License
description: License allowing users to copy and modify software, but requires the works to be distributed under the same license.

title: Genesis Block
description: The first ever block recorded on its respective blockchain network, also referred to as Block 0 or Block 1.

title: GitHub
description: A site/system/folder/repository where a team can share, collaborate, and save their open source or propietary code.

title: GM (Good Morning)
description: GM stands for "Good Morning," and its use in the crypto community reflects the inclusive and collaborative nature of the space.

title: Golden Cross
description: A bullish chart pattern where a shorter-term moving average crosses above a longer-term moving average.

title: Golden Ratio
description: A number that represents a special proportion found in nature, art, architecture, and even financial markets.

title: Goldilocks
description: An optimal state in economics where conditions are neither too hot nor too cold.

title: Gossip Protocol
description: A method of asynchronous peer-to-peer communication in a distributed system.

title: Gross Domestic Product (GDP)
description: A measure of the value of all the goods and services produced in a country during a specific time.

title: Gwei
description: A small denomination of ether, widely used as a measure of gas prices.

title: Hackathon
description: A blockchain hackathon is a competition where participants build sample applications atop a blockchain ecosystem.

title: Hacker
description: An individual that has an advanced understanding of computer systems and networks, including programmers and cybersecurity experts.

title: Haha Money Printer Go Brrrrr
description: Haha Money Printer Go Brrrrr is meme created in response to the Federal Reserve's 2020 plan to print money.

title: Halving
description: Halving is a process that reduces the block reward of a PoW crypto like Bitcoin (BTC) to half. The next halving of Bitcoin is expected around 2024.

title: Hard Cap
description: The maximum amount of funds a project intends to raise during their Initial Coin Offering (ICO) or alternative fundraising event.

title: Hard Landing
description: A situation where an economy rapidly transitions from growth to slow growth or recession.

title: Hash
description: The output produced by a hash function after a piece of data is mapped. May also be referred to as hash value, hash code, or digest.

title: Hash Rate
description: The speed at which a computer is able to calculate new hashes.

title: Hashed TimeLock Contract (HTLC)
description: Refers to a special feature that is used to create smart contracts that are able to modify payment channels.

title: Herd Instinct
description: The tendency of some traders to follow the actions of the majority.

title: High-Frequency Trading (HFT)
description: A type of algorithmic trading that involves the execution of a large number of orders in fractions of a second.

title: HODL
description: A typo of 'Hold' originating from bitcointalk that has also been retrofitted to be an acronym for Hold on for Dear Life - to maintain ownership of coins and not sell.

title: Honeypot
description: A mechanism used in computer security used to detect or counteract unauthorized access of information systems.

title: Iceberg Order
description: A conditional order to buy or sell a large amount of assets in smaller predetermined quantities in order to conceal the total order quantity.

title: Immutability
description: The inability to change or be changed. One of the core features behind Bitcoin and blockchain technology.

title: Index
description: A financial instrument used to track the price value of a given asset or basket of assets.

title: Index Funds
description: A type of investment fund designed to replicate the performance of a specific market index, such as the S&P 500.

title: Initial Coin Offering (ICO)
description: A fundraising method in which new projects will sell their cryptocurrency to investors.

title: Initial DEX Offering (IDO)
description: An Initial DEX Offering (IDO) is a type of blockchain fundraising method. It’s a public token sale conducted directly on a decentralized exchange (DEX).

title: Initial Exchange Offering (IEO)
description: A fundraising method designed to reduce the risk for token purchasers by introducing a trusted intermediary between the project team and the user.

title: Initial Public Offering (IPO)
description: Refers to the moment a private company starts offering its shares to the public for the first time.

title: Inscription
description: A piece of data attached to individual units of cryptocurrency.

title: Integrated Circuit (IC)
description: A small chip, typically made of silicon, that holds a set of electronic parts such as transistors, resistors or capacitors.

title: Interest Rates
description: Interest rates can refer either to the cost of borrowing money or the return earned on an investment, usually expressed as a percentage of the principal.

title: Interoperability
description: A concept of allowing blockchains to be compatible with each other and build upon each other's features and use-cases.

title: InterPlanetary File System (IPFS)
description: An open-source project building a protocol for distributed content storage and access.

title: IOU
description: An acronym which stands for “I owe you” and refers to an informal document that acknowledges a debt one party owes to another.

title: Isolated Margin
description: The margin balance allocated to a position. Traders can manage risk by restricting the amount allocated to each position.

title: Issuance
description: Generation of a new cryptocurrency which occurs in a variety of different ways, depending on parameters specified by the creators.

title: Keccak
description: A versatile cryptographic function designed by Guido Bertoni, Joan Daemen, Michaël Peeters, and Gilles Van Assche.

title: Know Your Customer (KYC)
description: A standard procedure in the finance industry which allows companies to identify their customers and comply with KYC AML laws.

title: Laspeyres Index
description: A tool used to track changes in the cost of a basket of goods over time.

title: Latency
description: The time between submitting a transaction to a network and the first confirmation of acceptance by the network.

title: Law of Demand
description: The law of demand relates to the willingness of consumers to buy a specific amount of goods or services for a particular price.

title: Layer 2
description: A secondary framework or protocol that is built on top of an existing blockchain.

title: Ledger
description: A physical book or a digital computer file where monetary and financial transactions are tracked and recorded.

title: Leveraged Tokens
description: Leveraged tokens are a type of cryptocurrency derivative that allows traders to gain leveraged exposure to a particular cryptocurrency without having to actively manage the margin requirements. They are designed to provide traders with a simplified way to trade leveraged positions in cryptocurrencies.

title: LFG
description: Stands for "Let’s F*ing Go" in crypto slang.

title: Library
description: A collection of stable resources, which may include executable files, documentation, message templates, and written code.

title: Lightning Network
description: A second layer operating on top of a blockchain, enabling increased transaction speed among participating nodes. This is one proposed scaling solution.

title: Limit Order
description: A type of order to buy or sell an asset at a specific price or better.

title: Linux
description: A popular open-source operating system, created in 1991 by Linus Torvalds. It is used in a wide range of devices around the world.

title: Liquid Staking
description: The process of tokenizing staked assets to provide enhanced liquidity.

title: Liquid Staking Token (LST)
description: A cryptocurrency token that represents staked assets on a blockchain network.

title: Liquidity
description: The ability to sell or buy any given asset without causing significant fluctuations in the market price for that asset.

title: Liquidity Crisis
description: A liquidity crisis is a financial situation in which an individual, organization, or market faces a shortage of cash, making it difficult to meet immediate financial obligations.

title: Liquidity Provider
description: Liquidity Providers (LPs) are entities or individuals who supply buy and sell orders to the financial markets to increase market liquidity and ensure a stable and efficient market.

title: Liquidity Ratios
description: Liquidity ratios are a type of financial metric used to measure a company’s capability to pay short-term obligations.

title: Listing
description: The addition of an asset to an exchange either as a request from the project team or as a decision made by the exchange.

title: Mainnet
description: A blockchain protocol which is fully developed and deployed where transactions are being broadcasted, verified, and recorded.

title: Mainnet Swap
description: When a coin migrates from a third party platform such as Ethereum or other token to a native on-chain token on their mainnet.

title: Maker
description: You become a “maker” when you place an order and it does not trade immediately, so your order stays in the order book and waits for someone else to fill/match with it later.

title: Malware
description: Any software program or code that is created to infiltrate and intentionally cause damage to computer systems and networks.

title: Margin Trading
description: Trading using borrowed funds - note: this is a high risk strategy and should only be done by experienced investors.

title: Market Capitalization
description: The total trading value of a given coin, calculated by the product of the supply of the coin by the current price.

title: Market Momentum
description: The ability of a certain market to maintain a continuous increase or decrease in price within a particular timeframe.

title: Market Order
description: When a taker picks the best available bid or ask for a cryptocurrency, taking the price and quantity available on the order book.

title: Masternode
description: Nodes on a network that often require a minimum amount of a given coin staked in order to access staking rewards.

title: Matching Engine
description: A matching engine is a piece of software designed to process trading orders, ensuring that buyers and sellers are paid correctly in financial markets.

title: Maximum Supply
description: Refers to the maximum number of coins or tokens that will be ever created for a given cryptocurrency.

title: Mempool
description: A node’s mechanism for keeping track of unconfirmed transactions that the node has seen (but have not yet been added to a block).

title: Merged Mining
description: The act of mining two or more cryptocurrencies at the same time, without sacrificing overall mining performance.

title: Merkle Tree
description: A way of organizing and structuring large amounts of data to make it more straightforward to process. A hash-based data structure.

title: Metadata
description: Data that includes information about other data, such as information about features of a specific transaction.

title: Metaverse
description: The metaverse is a concept of a persistent, online, 3D virtual environment that many believe will be a key element of future digital experiences.

title: Microtransactions
description: A small financial exchange using cryptocurrencies, such as bitcoin (BTC) or ether (ETH).

title: Mining
description: The verification of transactions on a blockchain network, in which transactions are added as entries into the blockchain ledger.

title: Mining Farm
description: A collection of many miners, often in a warehouse or large data center devoted to mining cryptocurrencies.

title: Minting
description: Minting adds fresh tokens into the crypto ecosystem, enabling them to be traded or used within its network. Minting is similar to mining, with some key differences.

title: Monetary Policy
description: Refers to the policies that authorities create and adopt to control the money supply and interest rates of a country.

title: Money Markets
description: Where people and institutions trade short-term financial instruments like T-bills and CDs.

title: Monitoring Tag
description: A tag used by Binance to classify assets that present higher volatility and risk when compared to other tokens.

title: Moon
description: A colloquial expression used to describe a cryptocurrency or other asset that is experiencing a strong upward market trend.

title: Moving Average Envelopes
description: A TA tool that consists of two bands placed around a core moving average.

title: Moving Average Ribbon
description: A technical analysis tool composed of multiple moving averages of varying lengths.

title: Mt. Gox
description: A defunct Bitcoin exchange based in Japan that was once the largest in the world.

title: Multisignature
description: Wallet which requires another party to authorize a transaction before it is broadcasted to the network.

title: Mutant Ape Yacht Club (MAYC)
description: An NFT collection by Yuga Labs that expands the Bored Ape ecosystem.

title: Nakamoto Consensus
description: A protocol used in blockchain networks to achieve agreement on the state of the blockchain without a central authority.

title: Net Asset Value (NAV)
description: The value of an entity’s total assets minus its liabilities.

title: NFT Floor Prices
description: Floor prices represent the lowest value at which you can purchase an NFT within a collection.

title: NFT Mystery Boxes
description: Non-fungible token (NFT) mystery boxes are a type of digital collectible that contain a random assortment of NFTs that are unknown the buyer until they open the box.

title: NGMI
description: NGMI is an acronym for "Not Gonna Make It," used to convey a sense of pessimism in achieving success or overcoming a challenge.

title: Node
description: A participant on a blockchain network that communicates with other participants to ensure the security and integrity of the system.

title: Non-Fungible Token (NFT)
description: A type of cryptographic token that represents a unique digital or real-world asset and isn't interchangeable.

title: Nonce
description: A single-use arbitrary string or number generated for verification purposes to prevent replaying past transactions.

title: OCO Order
description: It combines a limit order with a stop-limit order but only one of them can be executed.

title: Off-Chain
description: The processes and transactions that occur outside the main blockchain, often used to enhance scalability.

title: Offline Signing Orchestrator (OSO)
description: Offline Signing Orchestrator (OSO) is a technology developed by IBM to enhance the security of crypto and digital assets when they are stored in cold storage.

title: Offshore account
description: An account that is registered in a jurisdiction that is different to the jurisdiction of the holder's citizenship.

title: On-Chain
description: Transactions and activities directly recorded on the blockchain.

title: opBNB
description: opBNB acts as an important layer-2 scaling solution, which strategically offloads transaction processing and resource usage from the BNB Smart Chain.

title: Open-Source Software (OSS)
description: Software released under a license that gives anyone the ability and right to use, update, and distribute it freely.

title: Opportunity Cost
description: The value of what you miss out as a result of making a decision.

title: Oracle
description: A data source or feed from a third party used for determining outcomes for smart contracts.

title: ORC-20 Tokens
description: ORC-20 tokens run on the Bitcoin blockchain and are represented as JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) files inscribed onto satoshis with an Ordinal serial number, similar to BRC-20 tokens.

title: Order Book
description: An electronic list of outstanding buy and sell orders for a specific asset on an exchange or marketplace.

title: Ordinals
description: Ordinals are NFT-like entities that can be minted directly on the Bitcoin blockchain.

title: Orphan Block
description: A block whose parent block is unknown, formed in older versions of Bitcoin Core, where ancestry data wasn't required.

title: PancakeSwap
description: PancakeSwap is a decentralized exchange (DEX) that uses an AMM model. It's available in multiple blockchain networks with various DeFi products and services.

title: Paper Wallet
description: A piece of paper on which a cryptocurrency address and its corresponding private key are physically printed out.

title: Parallelization
description: A method for processing multiple transactions at the same time.

title: Passive Management
description: An investing strategy that doesn’t rely on active market exposure but rather tracks an existing economic index.

title: Peer-to-Peer (P2P)
description: When two or more computers are connected and share workload or resources without relying on a centralized server.

title: Pegged Currency
description: A currency whose value is tied to the value of another currency, commodity, or basket of currencies.

title: Permissionless Blockchain
description: Permissionless blockchains are open networks that allow anyone to participate in the consensus process without the need to obtain approval, permission, or authorization.

title: Phishing
description: A malicious attack where a bad actor will attempt to obtain the credentials of a user in order to gain unauthorised access into their account.

title: Plasma
description: An Ethereum off-chain scaling solution which may allow Etherum to greatly increase the transactions per second capablities.

title: Polkadot Crowdloan
description: Polkadot Crowdloan refers to the process of staking Polkadot (DOT) tokens to support specific projects in the Polkadot Slot Auction. In return, participants can receive rewards from the projects.

title: Ponzi Scheme
description: A scam in which new investors' funds are used to pay returns promised to previous investors. Do not invest in these.

title: Premining
description: When a portion of cryptocurrency tokens is mined or created before its official launch.

title: Price Action
description: The price movements of a financial asset over time. Plotted on a chart, it can be used by traders to identify trade setups.

title: Prisoner's Dilemma
description: An example that shows why two individuals might not cooperate, even if it may appear to be in their best interest.

title: Private Key
description: In cryptocurrency, a private key is a lengthy number that allows users to sign transactions and to generate receiving addresses.

title: Private Sale
description: An early stage investment round for strategic investors with a considerable amount of investible funds.

title: Profit and Loss (PnL)
description: Profit and Loss (PnL) is a measure of your gains or losses from your crypto trades.

title: Progressive Web application (PWA)
description: An application that is created through the use of modern web technologies and follows basic web standards.

title: Proof of Attendance Protocol (POAP)
description: A protocol that creates digital badges or collectibles to celebrate and record the attendance of an event.

title: Proof of Reserves (PoR)
description: Proof of Reserves (PoR) is a verification proving an exchange maintains funds to cover users' assets.

title: Proof of Stake (PoS)
description: A consensus mechanism that reward block validators according to the amount of coins they have at stake.

title: Proof of Staked Authority (PoSA)
description: Proof of Staked Authority merges Proof of Stake and Proof of Authority, enhancing blockchain security through its equal staking and balanced validation system.

title: Proof of Work (PoW)
description: Proof of Work is a crypto consensus mechanism where miners use special equipment to solve cryptographic puzzles to validate transactions and create new blocks.

title: Proposer-Builder Separation (PBS)
description: Proposer-builder separation (PBS) is an Ethereum concept aiming to improve scalability by dividing the responsibilities of block building into block proposers and block builders.

title: Proto-Danksharding
description: Proto-danksharding serves as a temporary scalability solution before the implementation of danksharding, which marks the final phase of Ethereum 2.0 (Serenity).

title: Pseudorandom
description: The property of a definite function that can produce an outcome that passes statistical tests of randomness.

title: Public Key
description: A public key is one-half of a key pair used to encrypt messages or verify digital signatures. In the crypto space, it essentially works as your wallet address.

title: Pump-and-dump
description: Pump-and-dumps are a deceptive practice used in crypto, where the price of a digital asset is artificially inflated and dumped, causing a sharp decline in the token value.

title: Quantitative Easing (QE)
description: A strategy used by central banks to boost the economy when it's not performing well.

title: Quantitative Tightening (QT)
description: A monetary policy tool used by central banks to reduce the supply of money in the economy.

title: Quantum Computing
description: Quantum computing works using particles that can be in superposition. These particles represent qubits instead of bits and can take the value of 1, 0 or both simultaneously.

title: Race attack
description: When two transactions are created with the same funds at the same time, with the intention of spending those funds twice.

title: Ransomware
description: A type of malware which takes over your computer and threatens to destroy or reveal files unless a ransom is paid.

title: Real World Assets (RWAs)
description: Real World Assets refer to tangible, physical assets with intrinsic value—like real estate, commodities, or art—that are tokenized for use on the blockchain.

title: Recession
description: An extended period of significant economic activity decline.

title: Recursive Inscription
description: A process where data or information is retrieved from existing inscriptions to generate new ones.

title: Rekt
description: A slang term used to define someone or something that has been destroyed or experienced catastrophic failure and a synonym for liquidated.

title: Relative Strength Index (RSI)
description: A technical indicator that measures market momentum & used to identify overbought and oversold conditions.

title: Resistance
description: A term in Technical Analysis (TA). When a price that is increasing finds resistance. Usually compared with previous highs.

title: Return on Investment (ROI)
description: A measure used in order to assess the efficiency of an investment. The ratio between net profit and net cost.

title: Revenge Trading
description: Psychological trap where traders try to quickly recover their losses.

title: Risk Premium
description: The additional compensation investors expect in return for taking on higher levels of risk.

title: Roadmap
description: A business planning technique which lays out the short and long term goals of a company within a flexible estimated timeline.

title: Routing Attack
description: An attack on the Internet Service Provider level to affect uptime or participation in a web-enabled system, such as a blockchain.

title: Rug pull
description: A type of crypto exit scam where a development team suddenly abandons a project.

title: Sahm Rule
description: An indicator used to identify the early stages of a recession.

title: Sandwich Trading
description: Sandwich trading, also known as sandwich attacks or sandwiching, is a trading strategy or manipulation technique in the cryptocurrency markets.

title: Satoshi
description: The smallest unit of a bitcoin, as defined by the Bitcoin protocol. It equals one-hundred-millionth of a bitcoin or 0.00000001 BTC.

title: Satoshi Nakamoto
description: The pseudonym of the creator or creators of the bitcoin protocol and whitepaper.

title: Secure Asset Fund for Users (SAFU)
description: Secure Asset Fund for Users is an emergency insurance fund created by Binance in 2018.

title: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)
description: An independent governmental agency responsible for regulating securities markets.

title: Security Audit
description: A systematic analysis to evaluate how safe a system, smart contract, or blockchain is agaisnt attacks or technical failures.

title: Security Token Offering (STO)
description: A fundraising method that involves the issuance of security tokens.

title: Seed Phrase
description: A seed phrase or mnemonic seed is a collection of words that can be used to access your cryptocurrency wallet.

title: Seed Tag
description: A tag used to classify riskier assets that are in their initial stages of development.

title: Segregated Witness (SegWit)
description: Segregated Witness, often abbreviated to SegWit, is an update to the Bitcoin protocol designed to address the network’s scalability and security issues.

title: Selfish Mining
description: The strategic withholding and releasing of blocks by a miner in order to gain a competitive advantage over the network.

title: Sell Wall
description: A very large limit sell order or a cumulation of sell orders at the same price level on an order book for an asset.

title: Sentiment
description: The overall attitude of a community in regards to a cryptocurrency or within investors towards a certain financial market.

title: Sharding
description: Sharding is a method of splitting blockchains ( or other types of databases) into smaller, partitioned blockchains that manage specific data segments.

title: Sharpe Ratio
description: A ratio created in 1966 that investors and economists use to assess the potential return of investment (ROI).

title: Sidechains
description: Sidechains are independent blockchains linked to a parent blockchain, designed to enhance scalability and facilitate digital asset transfers between networks.

title: Simple Moving Average (SMA)
description: A technical analysis tool that helps traders identify trends by smoothing out price data.

title: Slashing
description: A mechanism to punish malicious behavior in blockchains that use the Proof of Stake (PoS) consensus mechanism.

title: Slippage
description: When a trade settles at a different price than originally requested or expected, usually in low-liquidity markets and when using market orders.

title: Smart Contract
description: Smart contracts are self-executing contracts that exist on certain blockchain networks. Their conditions and terms are written directly into lines of code.

title: Smart Contract Wallet
description: Smart contract wallets are crypto wallets that leverage programmable smart contracts to introduce advanced functionalities and enhanced security features.

title: Snapshot
description: The ability to record the state of a blockchain ledger, storage device, or computer system at a specific point in time.

title: Social Recovery Wallet
description: Social recovery wallets are crypto wallets allowing crypto recovery. They utilize trusted contacts so users can regain access in case of lost keys or forgotten passwords.

title: Social Trading
description: Social trading is a strategy that enables individuals to mimic the trading behavior of expert investors and market professionals, often in real time.

title: SocialFi
description: SocialFi, short for Social Finance, represents a convergence of decentralized finance (DeFi) principles with the dynamics of social media.

title: Soft Landing
description: Soft landing refers to a situation in which the economy slows down gradually after a period of rapid growth while simultaneously avoiding a recession.

title: Solidity
description: Solidity is a programming language specially designed to write and implement smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain.

title: Source Code
description: Computer code, which is responsible for defining how software will function based on a list of instructions and statements.

title: SPL
description: SPL stands for Solana Program Library (SPL). It is a set of rules and protocols governing how tokens behave and interact on the Solana network.

title: SRC-20 Tokens
description: SRC-20 tokens leverage the Bitcoin Stamps technology to increase the utility of the Bitcoin blockchain.

title: Stablecoin
description: A type of cryptocurrency that is designed to maintain a stable value, rather than experiencing significant price changes.

title: Stagflation
description: An economic challenge that includes slow economic growth, higher scarcity of job offerings, and rising prices of goods and services.

title: Staking Pool
description: A pool where stakeholders combine their staking power to increase their chance of successfully validating a new block.

title: State Channel
description: A two-way communication channel between two users or nodes on a network, or between a user and a service.

title: stETH
description: A token designed to make Ethereum staking more inclusive and flexible for investors.

title: Stock Variable
description: The quantity of an economic resource measured at a specific point in time.

title: Store of Value
description: A commodity, asset or currency that can be saved, retrieved and exchanged at a future date, without suffering depreciation.

title: Supercomputer
description: A computer or virtual machine that operates at the highest level of currently possible computing power.

title: Supply Chain
description: A network of people and businesses involved in creating and distributing a particular product or serving a particular customer.

title: Support
description: A term in Technical Analysis (TA). When a price that is decreasing finds 'support'. Usually compared with lows.

title: Sybil Attack
description: A cyber attack where an entity creates and controls fake identities or nodes on a P2P network to gain influence.

title: Taker
description: The 'taker' is someone who decides to place an order that is instantly matched with an existing order on the order book.

title: Tank
description: A term adopted from the traditional financial markets and describes a strong negative financial performance of a particular asset.

title: Testnet
description: Replicas of the mainnet, offering risk-free, secure environments for exploring and testing.

title: The Graph (GRT)
description: A decentralized protocol focused on blockchain data accessibility.

title: Ticker Symbol
description: A short code that represents a stock, cryptocurrency, or other asset.

title: Token
description: Tokens (not to be confused with coins) are digital units issued on a blockchain. They can hold value or be redeemed for assets.

title: Token Generation Event (TGE)
description: A crowdfunding method used by blockchain projects and companies to launch tokens and raise funds.

title: Token Lockup
description: Token lockup or vesting period refers to the time span in which tokens or coins are not allowed to be transferred or traded.

title: Token Merge
description: When two or more crypto tokens are combined into a single, unified token.

title: Token Sale
description: The issuance of tokens in exchange for another cryptocurrency. Also referred to as an Initial Coin Offering.

title: Token Standards
description: Learn about ERC-20, ERC-721, BEP-20, and TRC-20 token standards in blockchain networks. Understand their role in cryptocurrency transactions and decentralized applications.

title: Tokenization
description: Tokenization refers to the process of converting real-world assets, such as real estate, art, or financial instruments, into digital tokens on a blockchain.

title: Tokenomics
description: The word “tokenomics” combines two words, ‘token’ and ‘economics.’ It allows us to see how a token functions, along with various factors that may affect its value.

title: Total Supply
description: Refers to the number of coins or tokens that currently exists and are either in circulation or locked somehow.

title: Total Value Locked (TVL)
description: Total Value Locked (TVL) is a widely used metric in the cryptocurrency industry that measures the total value of assets locked in a decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol.

title: TradFi
description: TradFi is the traditional financial system of banks and regulated institutions.

title: Transaction ID (TXID)
description: A transaction ID (TXID) is a unique string of characters that labels each transaction on the blockchain.

title: Transactions Per Second (TPS)
description: The number of transactions that a blockchain network is capable of processing each second.

title: Treasury Bills (T-Bills)
description: Financial instruments issued by governments to raise funds.

title: TrueUSD (TUSD)
description: TrueUSD is a stablecoin pegged to the U.S. dollar. It is integrated with multiple blockchain networks and is among the fastest-growing stablecoins.

title: Trustless
description: No single entity has authority over the system and consensus is achieved between participants who do not have to trust each other.

title: Turing Complete
description: A machine that, given enough time and memory along with the necessary instructions, can solve any computational problem, no matter how complex.

title: Understanding CZ’s Number 4
description: The number 4 represents one of CZ’s New Years resolutions to ignore FUD, fake news and attacks on Binance.

title: Unit of Account
description: One of the primary properties of money. Allowing measurement and comparison of the value of different things.

title: Unspent Transaction Output (UTXO)
description: An output created in a transaction, which must be referenced in a future transaction to spend funds.

title: User Interface (UI)
description: The interface where interactions between humans and machines occur. It establishes how a user can interact with a machine.

title: Utility Token
description: A digital token issued through a blockchain network, often via an initial coin offering (ICO), initial exchange offering (IEO) or token generation event (TGE).

title: Verification Code
description: Code sent to a second device to ensure the identity of someone logging in to an account. Used for Two-Factor Authentication.

title: Virtual Machine
description: An emulated computer system, or a distributed system that is designed to replicate the features of a computer's architecture.

title: Vladimir Club
description: A term used to describe someone who has acquired 1% of 1% (0.01%) of the maximum supply of a cryptocurrency.

title: Volatility
description: How quickly and how much the price of an asset changes. Calculated in terms of standard deviations in the annual return of an asset over a set period of time.

title: Volume
description: A measurement of the number of individual units of an asset that changed hands in a market during a given time.

title: WAGMI
description: An acronym for "We’re All Gonna Make It".

title: Wallet
description: Used to send and receive cryptocurrencies. Different types include software wallets, hardware wallets, and paper wallets.

title: Wash Trading
description: An unethical practice to create false market activity.

title: Weak Hands
description: A term referring to traders or investors who lack the confidence to hold their assets or to follow their trading plans.

title: Weak Subjectivity
description: Relates to the need for certain nodes to rely on other nodes when determining the current state of a PoS blockchain.

title: Web 1.0
description: The initial iteration of the web, when data was primarily read-only pages connected with hyperlinks. Also known as 'read-only' web.

title: Wei
description: The smallest possible denomination of ether (ETH), the currency used on the Ethereum network. Often used when referring to gas prices.

title: Weighted Moving Average (WMA)
description: A technical analysis tool with greater sensitivity to recent price changes.

title: Whale
description: An individual or organization that holds a large amount of Bitcoins or other cryptocurrency, allowing them to impact the markets.

title: Whiskers
description: The lines extending from the colored bar in a candlestick chart that indicate the full low-high range of a trading pair within a certain time frame. Also referred to as wicks or shadows.

title: Whitelist
description: A list of allowed or trusted individuals, computer programs, or cryptocurrency addresses in relation to a service or event.

title: Wick
description: A line found on a candlestick chart which is used to indicate where the price of an asset is fluctuating in regards to its opening and closing prices.

title: Win Rate
description: In financial markets, win rate (or win ratio) is a metric that indicates how profitable a trader might be.

title: Wrapped Ether (WETH)
description: ERC-20 token representing ether at a 1:1 ratio.

title: Wyckoff
description: The Wyckoff method, created by Richard Wyckoff in the 1930s, is an approach to trading and investing that caused a lasting impact on modern technical analysis.

title: Yield Farming
description: Yield farming is a high-risk practice in DeFi where investors lock up assets to provide liquidity, lend or stake in return for rewards or interest.

title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs
description: Proofs to verify that transactions are valid without revealing any information about these transactions, providing privacy to the transaction while maintaining its legitimacy.

title: Zk-rollup
description: Zk-rollup is a layer-2 scaling solution designed to increase the transaction throughput of blockchain networks without compromising on security.

title: zk-SNARKs
description: “Zero-Knowledge Succinct Non-Interactive Argument of Knowledge” - an approach to zero-knowledge proofs.

title: zk-STARKs
description: zk-STARKs allow one party (the prover) to communicate validated data or execute computations with a third party without the data or analysis being revealed to the counterparty (the verifier).
